status=open
reason=Bash tool is unusable in this execution environment — every invocation fails immediately with "ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-14de66ea-20260708T175024-6350a08c'" before any command runs, including trivial ones like `echo hi` or `df -h`. This was reproduced twice: once directly, and once via a fresh general-purpose subagent hitting the identical error, confirming the disk-full condition is at the host/container level, not specific to this session's shell state. Without shell access I cannot run `go build`, `go test`, `git`, or `lefthook run pre-commit`, all of which are mandatory per the execute-bead contract before any commit. I read the three files named in the bead (cli/cmd/work_status.go, cli/cmd/worker.go, cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go) and confirmed the described gap exists (worker.go's `status` command reads only WorkerDesiredState and never consults terminal worker history recorded by workers_supervisor.go's recordTerminalHistory/blockedTerminals; work_status.go never surfaces desired-vs-live deltas at all), but did not write code changes because they could not be compiled, tested, or committed in this environment. Retry once the host disk-full condition clears; no code-level blocker exists.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes

before any command runs, including trivial ones like `echo hi` or `df -h`. This was reproduced twice: once
directly, and once via a fresh general-purpose subagent hitting the identical error, confirming the disk-full
condition is at the host/container level, not specific to this session's shell state.

Without shell access I cannot run `go build`, `go test`, `git`, or `lefthook run pre-commit`, all of which are
mandatory per the execute-bead contract before any commit. I read the three files named in the bead
(cli/cmd/work_status.go, cli/cmd/worker.go, cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go) and confirmed the
described gap exists (worker.go's `status` command reads only WorkerDesiredState and never consults terminal
worker history recorded by workers_supervisor.go's recordTerminalHistory/blockedTerminals; work_status.go never
surfaces desired-vs-live deltas at all), but did not write code changes because they could not be compiled,
tested, or committed in this environment.

Retry once the host disk-full condition clears; no code-level blocker exists.

status=open
reason=Bash tool unusable in this execution worktree — every invocation fails with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/...'`, including trivial commands like `true` and `echo hi`. This is host/sandbox-level disk or inode exhaustion in /tmp, not a defect in the ddx repository. Read/Edit/Write tools work (used to inspect cli/cmd/work_status.go, cli/cmd/worker.go, and cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go for this bead), but go build/go test/ lefthook all require a shell, so the required verification for AC4-6 cannot be run. Per the execute-bead contract, no commit should land without passing tests, so no code changes were made this attempt. Retry on a worker with a healthy /tmp (or once this sandbox's disk/inode pressure clears) — this is an environment condition, not a repo state that needs further changes before retry. </content>
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes

</content>

status=blocked
reason=Host /tmp is completely full (ENOSPC). Every Bash tool invocation fails at "mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/...'" before any command runs, including trivial ones like `true` or `df -h`. This blocks running `cd cli && go test ...`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, and even read-only `grep` needed to locate the fd-exhaustion structured fields referenced by the bead (agent/server fd-exhaustion emission path). Read/Write/ Edit tools still work (no /tmp dependency), so this is not a repo-state or code issue — it is host disk exhaustion outside the worktree's control. Retry once /tmp has free space; no source changes were made in this attempt because none could be verified by the required go test / lefthook gates.
suggested_action=recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared
